introSection: Scenic, or Scenic Railway, was the first roller coaster to be built at Six Gun Territory, at the time known as Willow Grove Park, and operated until the park's closure in 1975. The side friction roller coaster was designed and built by famed coaster and ride builder, William Morris. The Scenic Railway was often referred to as the "Nickel Scenic" referring to the cost of 5 cents per ride. This price remained the same throughout the operating life of the roller coaster.

sections.title: History
sections.content: After standing but not operating since the park's closure, the ride was demolished in 1980.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Riders would depart the station and embark on a lift hill that would then lead to an out and back design course through the trees in a wooded area of the park. At the time, this was considered a novel use of the newly patented scenic railway and side friction roller coasters which were commonly built in the figure 8 style.

fullText:
Scenic, or Scenic Railway, was the first roller coaster to be built at Six Gun Territory, at the time known as Willow Grove Park, and operated until the park's closure in 1975. The side friction roller coaster was designed and built by famed coaster and ride builder, William Morris. The Scenic Railway was often referred to as the "Nickel Scenic" referring to the cost of 5 cents per ride. This price remained the same throughout the operating life of the roller coaster.

After standing but not operating since the park's closure, the ride was demolished in 1980.

Riders would depart the station and embark on a lift hill that would then lead to an out and back design course through the trees in a wooded area of the park. At the time, this was considered a novel use of the newly patented scenic railway and side friction roller coasters which were commonly built in the figure 8 style.
